text: Planners should not pre-check the critic checklist in progress.md — it signals review-complete when the critic hasn't run yet. Reset unchecked before critic iteration.
After a builder fix iteration, update progress.md status lines before handing to critic — stale 'fix needed' labels cause confusion about current state.

When qa.failed routes to inspector and all surfaces are already fully validated with failures fully characterized, progress.md should explicitly guide the inspector toward task.complete rather than re-inspection. This prevents unnecessary extra cycles.

Catalog-driven differential coverage can currently enforce 91 of 99 active compileable fixtures; the remaining 8 need explicit exclusions for tn_runtime_for gaps, multi-clause anon capture, or native diagnostic text drift.
When the loop objective changes or a slice completes, refresh .miniloop/context.md and .miniloop/plan.md immediately; stale docs can pull the strategist into unrelated work.
Catalog-driven differential selection plus explicit exclusion reasons is a good backpressure pattern: every compileable parity fixture must be enforced or intentionally acknowledged.
Once a slice already sits on task.complete with aligned context/plan/progress, the next loop turn should stop cleanly instead of re-verifying the same evidence.
After task.complete, advisory topology may still show generic next roles/events; treat that as stale routing metadata and stop unless scope or evidence changed.
